surface_verification.justification: Test-only CI guardrail (a policy check over .trivyignore.yaml). No product web/api/cli/UI/store surface is introduced or changed. The guardrail is self-tested by 4 tests run CI-style (uv run --with pytest pytest shared/tests/...): good register passes, sloppy entry rejected, absent file passes.

degraded: Full monorepo + shared/tests suites delegated to CI ci.yml. This change is purely additive (one new test file in shared/tests/, no source/runtime code touched), so blast radius is zero. Local gate = the new policy test (4 passed, CI-style invocation) + ruff clean.

behaviors.behavior: the policy rejects a sloppy accept — an entry missing id / expired_at / statement, or with no paths|purls scope (repo-wide blanket suppression)
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_trivyignore_register.py::test_policy_rejects_a_sloppy_accept PASSED (asserts _violations flags the 3 bad shapes and accepts a well-formed one)

behaviors.behavior: the current repo-root .trivyignore.yaml passes the policy (CVE-2026-54285 is scoped + time-bounded + justified) and is well-formed YAML
behaviors.evidence: test_accepts_are_scoped_timebound_and_justified + test_register_is_well_formed_if_present PASSED against the real register

behaviors.behavior: the register is OPTIONAL: an absent .trivyignore.yaml yields no violations (no false CI failure on a fresh checkout or a fully-expired-and-removed register)
